sanskrit:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते।
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते॥
transliteration:
prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṃ hānir asyopajāyate |
prasanna-cetaso hy āśu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣṭhate ||
literal_meaning: By grace / all / of sorrows / destruction / of this / arises; / of the serene-minded / indeed / quickly / the intellect / becomes steady.
contextual_meaning: When the mind is anchored in inner serenity (prasāda), the entire spectrum of psychological pain dissolves; clarity of judgment returns almost immediately.
psychological_state: Post-regulation homeostasis: the nervous system shifts from fight-freeze to ventral-vagal safety, allowing prefrontal cortex to re-engage.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack, you step outside, feel the sun on your face, and suddenly the catastrophic thoughts lose their grip—your mind feels usable again.
anchor_text: Let inner calm dissolve sorrow; steady wisdom follows a tranquil mind.
